page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64as_v7 demonstrates strong compute capabilities, particularly in single-core tasks. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression all rank in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth (both read and write) consistently place in the top 25% tier. For database operations, the PassMark database score is in the top 10%, while Redis SET operations reach 94.83 million operations per second, placing it in the top 25%. However, a tradeoff is observed in large-scale language model inference; while text generation for a medium 7B model ranks in the top 10%, prompt processing and text generation for a large 70B model fall into the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64as_v7 offers a high density of dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources and memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized GPU hardware. This makes it a resource-efficient option for workloads that rely on external network-attached storage. The instance is suited for enterprise application hosting, relational databases, web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference tasks that do not require hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 x86_64 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth results. However, large LLM inference speeds remain average. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. It achieves 94.83 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, making it highly capable for memory-intensive operations despite the lack of local NVMe drives. This server is suited for database hosting, web serving, and general enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core and database benchmark performance, suited for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering balanced compute and memory resources for enterprise databases and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU, gzip compression, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU, optimized for external Premium Storage configurations
Suited for enterprise application hosting, relational databases, and web serving
